primarySources: NIST SP 800-61 Rev. 2 — Incident Handling Guide
provenance.validationProcess: Practitioner-authored against primary regulatory text; production-tested in live governance workflows; human-in-the-loop review at every authoring stage. Not audited by a third-party compliance body. Not reviewed by legal counsel.
provenance.disclaimer: Governance scaffolding for education and drafting. Not legal advice. Not a substitute for qualified professional judgment.

entries.description: Classifies an AI system's risk tier under the EU AI Act (prohibited, high-risk, limited-risk, minimal-risk) and outputs a tailored compliance obligations checklist scoped to the deployer role — provider, deployer, importer, or distributor.
representativeQueries: classify my AI system under the EU AI Act
is my AI product high-risk under EU AI Act Article 6
what are my EU AI Act compliance obligations as a deployer
do I need a conformity assessment for my AI system
what risk category does my AI fall into under EU regulation

entries.description: Walks an AI project team through the NIST AI Risk Management Framework (AI RMF 1.0) at any lifecycle stage. Produces a stage-scoped risk profile with addressed and unaddressed controls across the Govern, Map, Measure, and Manage functions.
representativeQueries: run a NIST AI RMF risk assessment on my AI system
what NIST AI RMF controls are we missing for our AI governance program
check our AI risk management posture against NIST AI RMF 1.0
build a risk profile for my AI project using the NIST framework
NIST AI RMF Govern Map Measure Manage function checkpoint

entries.description: Guides teams through an ISO/IEC 42001:2023 AI Management System clause-by-clause audit readiness check. Produces scoped gap analysis reports, assigns control owners, flags audit evidence requirements, and generates documentation stubs ready for auditor review.
representativeQueries: prepare for an ISO 42001 AI management system certification audit
ISO 42001 clause-by-clause gap analysis for our AI program
what audit evidence do we need for ISO 42001 certification
check our AI management system readiness against ISO 42001
generate documentation stubs for ISO 42001 compliance

entries.description: Inserts a structured human-in-the-loop compliance review checkpoint into any AI workflow. Generates a sign-off checklist covering EU AI Act, NIST AI RMF, and ISO 42001 obligations that a human reviewer must clear before an AI output is approved to proceed.
representativeQueries: add a human review checkpoint before my AI output is approved
run a compliance gate check before shipping AI-generated content
generate a human sign-off checklist for AI workflow approval
is this AI output safe to proceed under EU AI Act and NIST
compliance checkpoint covering EU AI Act NIST and ISO 42001

metadata.framework: EU AI Act, NIST AI RMF, ISO 42001

entries.description: Generates ready-to-publish AI transparency notices, model cards, and system disclosures. Produces EU AI Act Article 13 notices for high-risk AI deployers and NIST AI RMF-aligned model cards — actual draft documents ready to edit and publish, not checklists.
representativeQueries: write an EU AI Act Article 13 transparency notice for my AI system
generate a model card for my AI deployment
create a transparency disclosure for users of my AI product
draft an AI system card aligned to NIST AI RMF Govern function
what do I need to disclose about my AI system under the EU AI Act

metadata.framework: EU AI Act Article 13, NIST AI RMF Govern

entries.description: Audits AI system data inputs, outputs, training datasets, and pipelines for PII exposure risks under GDPR and EU AI Act Article 10 data governance requirements. Produces a structured exposure report with per-finding risk ratings and specific remediation actions.
representativeQueries: check my AI training data and pipeline for PII exposure risks
audit my AI system for personal data under GDPR
does my dataset contain sensitive personal information I should not be processing
EU AI Act Article 10 data governance compliance audit
scan AI inputs and outputs for privacy risks before deployment

entries.description: Structures AI incident reports aligned to NIST SP 800-61 incident response phases and EU AI Act Article 62 serious incident reporting obligations. Identifies whether the incident meets the Article 62 serious incident threshold and routes the report to the correct internal and regulatory stakeholders.
representativeQueries: structure an AI incident report for regulatory submission
does this AI system failure meet the EU AI Act Article 62 serious incident threshold
generate a post-incident review report for an AI failure aligned to NIST SP 800-61
log and route an AI bias or harmful output incident for compliance review
AI incident debrief documentation for internal and regulator review

metadata.framework: NIST SP 800-61, EU AI Act Article 62

entries.description: Weekly publishing operating system for music publishers — orchestrates the PUBLISH Loop by routing each task to the right skill in the right order, with human-in-the-loop checkpoints across the weekly cadence.
representativeQueries: run the weekly publishing OS
what should I be working on this week
run the PUBLISH Loop
orchestrate my publishing tasks
what is due in my publishing operation this week

create a provenance record for my music

entries.description: REST approval queue that sits between an AI agent and any action requiring human sign-off. Agents POST pending events with context; human reviewers approve or reject via PATCH. Every decision is timestamped and stored for 30 days, creating a verifiable audit trail for EU AI Act Article 14, NIST AI RMF MANAGE 1.2, and ISO 42001 §9.1 human oversight requirements.
representativeQueries: queue an AI decision for human approval before the agent proceeds
REST API to hold AI-generated output pending human review
approve or reject a pending AI agent action via API
generate an audit trail for human-in-the-loop AI decisions
enforce human oversight on AI workflows via approval queue

metadata.auth: Bearer token required for all endpoints (read and write)
metadata.framework: EU AI Act Article 14, NIST AI RMF MANAGE 1.2, ISO 42001 §9.1
